lever_summary: Extend the pass65 dense-F64 axis-reduction odometer to the remaining eval_reduce_axes branches (integral i64, complex, non-dense float fallback) via a shared OutIndexOdometer. Replaces the dtype-independent per-element multi-index decode (flat_to_multi_into Vec allocation + multi_to_out_flat kept-axes loop, per element) with an incremental out_idx advanced in O(1) amortized. i64 (and complex) have no dense storage, so this isolates the decode-removal win; dense_f64_axis_reduce was refactored onto the same odometer.

alien_graveyard.primitive: strength reduction of per-element address arithmetic (incremental index odometer)
alien_graveyard.mapping: carry-propagating odometer replaces full multi-index decode + recomputation that was O(rank) work plus a heap allocation per element

method: Same-worker A/B on ts2 confirmed by an unchanged calibration bench (eval/reduce_sum_64k_f64, a full reduction this change does not touch): decode-baseline calib 76353.1ns vs odometer calib 75718.2ns = 0.8% worker-speed delta, so the i64 ratios need no normalization. Baseline produced by git-stashing reduction.rs back to pass65 (decode loops) and rebuilding.
command: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- 'reduce_sum_256_axis._i64|reduce_sum_64k_f64$' --save-baseline {cB|cA}

change.lever: Added struct OutIndexOdometer { dims, out_axis_stride, coord, out_idx } with next_index(); the complex, integral, and float-fallback loops in eval_reduce_axes iterate tensor.elements.iter() and call odometer.next_index() instead of flat_to_multi_into + multi_to_out_flat. The now-unused `strides` binding in eval_reduce_axes was removed (flat_to_multi_into / multi_to_out_flat / checked_strides remain used by the bitwise and cumulative paths).

isomorphism.ordering: unchanged: tensor.elements.iter() visits elements in ascending flat order (identical to `for flat_idx in 0..total`); each output cell accumulates its inputs in the same sequence
isomorphism.index_mapping: OutIndexOdometer.next_index() reproduces multi_to_out_flat(flat_to_multi_into(flat)) for flat = 0,1,2,...: out_axis_stride is row-major over out_dims ordered by kept_axes, reduced axes contribute 0, innermost axis varies fastest
isomorphism.floating_point: unchanged: same float_init/int_init seeds and the same caller ops (int_op, float_op, complex component-wise float_op, complex_lex_cmp for max/min, complex prod via (re,im) cross terms) applied in the same order; no reassociation
isomorphism.tie_breaking: unchanged (complex max/min still use complex_lex_cmp / total_cmp)
isomorphism.errors: elements.iter() yields exactly product(dims) items (TensorValue invariant) so out_idx stays within 0..out_count; the same as_i64()/as_f64()/literal_to_complex_parts type checks run per element and return the same errors; full-axis reductions still delegate to eval_reduce before this branch
isomorphism.output_dtype: unchanged: integral -> I64, complex -> input dtype via complex_literal_from_parts, float -> reduce_real_output_dtype/reduce_real_literal

validation.name: reduction_parity_and_unit_tests
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax --lib reduction
validation.result: passed: 30 tests (incl. dense_f64_axis_reduce_bit_identical_to_literal_path and the value-anchored reduce_axes_sum_rows/cols/3d tests covering integral + float odometer outputs)

validation.name: clippy_deny_warnings
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-lax --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings
